case_title: ADAM CLARK VS. THOMAS STATHAKES ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF CATHERINE WALSH IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO STRIKE (TRANSACTION ID # 100039271) FILED BY DEFENDANT STATHAKES, THOMAS
OBJECTION HEARING OF JAN-30-2019 HELD. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (610)
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR NOV-04-2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. NOTICE SENT BY COURT. (610)
ORDER GRANTING EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND ALL RELATED DEADLINES (COPY IN DEPT. 206 OUTBOX)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ADAM CLARK'S MOTION FOR ORDER PERMITTING DISCOVERY OF DEFENDANT THOMAS STATHAKE'S FINANCIAL CONDITION IS TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 301 ON THE COURT'S DISCOVERY CALENDAR AT 9:00 A.M. ON THE SAME DATE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THOMAS STATHAKES' MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ADAM CLARK IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATION THAT MR. STATHAKES KNOWINGLY DROVE A CAR WHILE HE WAS INTOXICATED IS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT PUNITIVE DAMAGES LIABILITY PER THE STATUTORY DEFINITION OF MALICE. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-05-2020 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant Thomas Stathake'S Financial Condition
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF ADAM CLARK'S Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant Thomas Stathake's Financial Condition is transferred to be heard in department 301 on the court's discovery calendar at 9:00 a.m. on the same date.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant Thomas Stathake'S Financial Condition
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant Thomas Stathake'S Financial Condition.

Off-calendar. Per request of Plaintiff's counsel, the motion is placed off calendar pursuant to a pending settlement.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Continued To Jun-29-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Set For Trial On Nov-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Set For Trial On Jul-29-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
H. Nathan James, Esq., Seifer, Murken, Despina, James & Teichman, Telephone 415-749-5900, appearing for Plaintiff;
Catherine A. Walsh, Esq., Telephone 925-825-9500, appearing for Defendant Thomas Stathakes (CourtCall);

RULING - ARGUED: No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Thomas Stathakes' motion to strike the request for exemplary damages in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Adam Clark is denied. The allegation that Mr. Stathakes knowingly drove a car while he was intoxicated is sufficient to support punitive damages liability per the statutory definition of malice. Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
